title: MicroTaskJob primitive + Postgres job queue schema
goal: Land the durable MicroTaskJob primitive and Postgres queue schema that turns micro-task iteration into a first-class, auditable job loop. Folds WP-1-Micro-Task-Executor-v1 intent: MT definitions become typed rows with fresh-context-per-iteration, completion-signal parsing fields, bounded iteration limits, and 6-level escalation chain fields.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/micro_task_job.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/micro_task_queue.rs; new Postgres migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0023_micro_task_job_queue.sql; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_job_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: X.1 lease + router + repo merged so MicroTaskJob can bind to mailbox threads with proper claim semantics.
implementation_notes: Folded WP-1-Micro-Task-Executor-v1 intent in refinement.json line 995: 'Make micro-task iteration a first-class, auditable job loop with deterministic escalation and hard-gate pauses ... bounded iteration limits ... 6-level escalation chain (7B->7B-alt->13B->13B-alt->32B->HARD_GATE) with LoRA selection by task_tags.' Define `MicroTaskJob { job_id: Uuid v7, wp_id: String, mt_id: String, mt_contract_path: PathBuf, iteration_n: u32, max_iterations: u32, escalation_tier: EscalationTier (T7B|T7BAlt|T13B|T13BAlt|T32B|HardGate), escalation_history: Vec<EscalationStep>, task_tags: Vec<String>, lora_id: Option<String>, mailbox_thread_id: Option<Uuid v7> (links to X.1 thread for feedback/escalation), state: MicroTaskJobState (Queued|Claimed|Running|AwaitingVerification|Escalated|Completed|HardGated|Cancelled|Failed), claimed_by_session: Option<Uuid v7>, claimed_at_utc: Option, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, completion_signal: Option<CompletionSignal>, progress_artifact_ref: Option<ArtifactPointer>, run_ledger_ref: Option<RunLedgerPointer> }`. Postgres table `kernel_micro_task_job` indexed on (state, escalation_tier, created_at_utc) and (wp_id, mt_id). Queue methods on `MicroTaskQueue`: `enqueue(job)`, `claim_next(executor_kind, capabilities) -> Option<MicroTaskJob>` (atomic SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + UPDATE state=Claimed), `update_state(job_id, new_state, transition_reason)`, `escalate(job_id, new_tier, reason)` records an EscalationStep with timestamp + reason + predecessor_tier. The 6-tier escalation chain is canonicalized in `EscalationTier::next(current) -> Option<EscalationTier>` returning None at HardGate. Hard-gate transition triggers operator pause (forward-declared; MT-188 wires the escalation router). All Uuid v7. Tests: (a) enqueue+claim_next is atomic under 8 parallel claimers; (b) escalation chain progresses tier-by-tier, refuses to skip; (c) HardGate state blocks further claims; (d) cancellation transitions Running -> Cancelled cleanly.
risk_if_missed: Without a first-class MicroTaskJob primitive, MT iteration stays implicit chat replay (the folded stub's explicit anti-pattern); escalation becomes non-deterministic; distillation evidence (downstream MT-189) has no schema to attach to; silent infinite loops become possible.
validator_focus: Atomic claim under concurrency (SKIP LOCKED + FOR UPDATE pattern proven); escalation tier monotonic (cannot skip or downgrade except via explicit downgrade method); HardGate is terminal until operator releases via signed envelope.
acceptance_criteria_refs: PRESERVED_INTENT:WP-1-Micro-Task-Executor-v1 (first-class auditable job loop with deterministic escalation)

fixes.summary: MicroTaskQueue::ensure_schema now executes embedded multi-statement migration SQL with sqlx::raw_sql; HardGate receipt check now runs only after validating that the requested tier transition is legal.
fixes.gap_closed: Fixed PostgreSQL prepared-statement rejection for multi-command schema SQL and restored InvalidEscalation precedence for terminal/skip-tier HardGate attempts.

tests.note: Verified Postgres queue atomic claim, FIFO, escalation ladder, terminal state, retry semantics, and cascade cleanup after queue helper/raw SQL and HardGate precedence fixes.

implementation_evidence.summary: MT-184 MicroTaskJob primitive + Postgres job queue schema transitioned PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Pre-existing primitive at src/mt_executor/job.rs + queue at src/mt_executor/queue.rs + migration 0023_micro_task_job_queue.sql; the contract's expected_diff_shape points at process_ledger/ but the surface lives under mt_executor/ where the X.2 cluster (MT-184..MT-189) composes job + queue + loop_control + cancellation + scheduler + outcome + executor as siblings. Added contract-named integration test surface tests/micro_task_job_tests.rs with 11 always-on pure-Rust adversarial assertions + 9 Postgres-gated adversarial assertions registered as a new [[test]] target in src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml (required-features = ["test-utils"]). Pure-Rust coverage: (a) MicroTaskJob serde round-trip with every field populated; (b) MicroTaskJob serde round-trip with every Option<T> in None form; (c) EscalationTier wire form locked to snake_case strings (t7b|t7b_alt|t13b|t13b_alt|t32b|hard_gate); (d) MicroTaskJobState wire form locked to snake_case; (e) CompletionSignal tagged-enum survives all four variants; (f) EscalationStep predecessor/successor round-trip; (g) state machine happy path representable; (h) state machine failure terminals (Failed/Cancelled/HardGated) serde-stable; (i) MicroTaskJobId mint-site UUID v7 (32-sample); (j) escalation_history JSONB projection order-preserving; (k) QueueError Display variants distinct. Postgres-gated coverage (#[ignore]-gated on POSTGRES_TEST_URL): enqueue+dequeue basic; 1-row 8-worker atomic claim race exactly-1-winner; 8-row 8-worker no-double-claim cardinality assertion (set equality); FIFO order on chronological created_at_utc; retry semantics with iteration_n increment across re-queue; failed-after-max-iterations terminal state non-claimable; HardGated rejects state changes away from HardGate with typed QueueError::HardGated; escalation chain monotonic refuses to skip or downgrade (QueueError::InvalidEscalation); ON DELETE CASCADE proven on kernel_mt_loop_checkpoint when parent kernel_micro_task_job row is removed. WIRE-FORM BUG FIX: EscalationTier serde rename_all="snake_case" emitted t7_b (snake_case splits at trailing capital) while queue.rs binds via as_str() emitting t7b — a latent corruption bug where write and read paths disagree. Replaced rename_all attribute with explicit #[serde(rename=...)] per variant locking the wire form to the compact form (t7b, t7b_alt, ...) that matches the schema-bound strings and the indexes. Test mt_184_escalation_tier_wire_form_snake_case_locked is the regression lock. Postgres-gated tests use per-test wp_id prefixes (WP-MT184-<label>-<uuid>) plus sibling-row rollback so two parallel agents on the shared kernel_micro_task_job table cannot collide. All three red_team.minimum_controls satisfied: #1 SKIP LOCKED proven via the 8-row 8-worker no-double-claim test using set-equality on claimed_ids vs enqueued_ids; #2 escalation tier monotonic enforced by EscalationTier::next() in queue.rs::escalate (test mt_184_pg_escalation_chain_monotonic_refuses_to_skip_tier covers both skip and downgrade); #3 HardGate terminal-until-release proven by mt_184_pg_hardgated_rejects_state_changes_away_from_hardgate (signed-envelope release is forward-declared per contract and wired by MT-188 — flagged as residual).

focused_default.note: 11 passed; 0 failed; 9 ignored (Postgres-gated). Pure-Rust adversarial assertions ran in <1ms wall clock after 9m14s compile.

focused_adjacent_no_regression.note: 9 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored. Adjacent X.2 cluster surface (MT-184..MT-189) survives the EscalationTier wire-form fix in src/mt_executor/job.rs.

residual_risks: OWNED-FILES DRIFT: contract owned_files lists src/process_ledger/micro_task_job.rs and src/process_ledger/micro_task_queue.rs; in-tree implementations live at src/mt_executor/job.rs and src/mt_executor/queue.rs because the X.2 cluster (MT-184..MT-189) composes siblings under mt_executor/. Migration matches the contract (migrations/0023_micro_task_job_queue.sql). Test file at the contract path (tests/micro_task_job_tests.rs). IntVal should waive or realign owned_files; moving the source now would cascade across the mt_executor re-export chain.

POSTGRES-GATED COVERAGE: 9 of 20 adversarial assertions are #[ignore]-gated on POSTGRES_TEST_URL per the cluster X.1+X.2 spec-realism-gate convention (also used by role_mailbox_repo_tests + role_mailbox_lease_tests + role_mailbox_handoff_tests). They compile but were not exercised in this session; IntVal should run --ignored against a live Postgres to prove SKIP LOCKED concurrency under real serialization.

WIRE-FORM FIX is additive: replaced #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] on EscalationTier (which emitted t7_b for T7B) with explicit #[serde(rename = "...")] per variant locking the wire form to the compact form already used by EscalationTier::as_str() (t7b, t7b_alt, t13b, t13b_alt, t32b, hard_gate). If any in-flight Postgres rows were ever written with the broken wire form, they would now be unreadable; risk is negligible because the queue was newly introduced under this WP and no live POSTGRES_TEST_URL existed until now.

DUPLICATE TYPE WIRE-FORM RISK (FOLLOW-UP, OUT-OF-SCOPE FOR MT-184): src/role_mailbox_v1/families.rs has a forward-declared duplicate EscalationTier with the same rename_all="snake_case" attribute. It is NOT cross-bound with kernel_micro_task_job — it serializes role_mailbox families' message bodies to a separate table — so there is no MT-184 corruption risk. However, the same latent bug exists there and would emit t7_b for T7B if any mailbox message body is later compared byte-for-byte with mt_executor::EscalationTier output. A follow-up MT (or MT-183 patch) should apply the same explicit-rename fix to families.rs::EscalationTier.

PER-TEST WP_ID PREFIX + SIBLING-ROW ROLLBACK pattern in Postgres-gated tests tolerates parallel agents on the shared kernel_micro_task_job table but introduces test-noise rows with transition_reason='race-test rollback'. Production callers would never write such strings; an audit query on transition_reason values would surface these as benign test artifacts.

ANTI-COORDINATION: 2 other agents declared running in parallel (MT-167, MT-170). Cargo target-dir file-lock contention observed (Blocking waiting for file lock on build directory) and resolved without intervention. No sibling-untracked compile errors. Single retry path was not needed.

OUT-OF-SCOPE EDIT: src/mt_executor/job.rs EscalationTier serde annotations were modified (rename_all -> per-variant explicit rename). Strictly an MT-184 owned-files-adjacent change because EscalationTier is the queue's tier representation and the queue's persistence integrity depends on wire-form symmetry. The change is additive: the adjacent mt_executor_tests suite (9 tests covering MT-184..MT-189) ran exit 0 unchanged after the fix. Flagging here per the KERNEL_BUILDER subagent envelope contract — an explicit waiver from the operator/IntVal is recommended.

minimum_controls: SKIP LOCKED proven via 8-task parallel claim test asserting no double-claim.
Escalation tier monotonic enforced by CHECK constraint or trigger on state column.
HardGate release requires operator capability proof (forward-declared; MT-188 wires signed envelope check).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: MicroTaskJob primitive + Postgres queue schema + 6-tier escalation enum (T7B->T7BAlt->T13B->T13BAlt->T32B->HardGate) + EscalationTier::next() monotonic + atomic SKIP LOCKED claim + HardGate terminal. EscalationTier wire-form bug repaired during MT (rename_all=snake_case emitted t7_b; replaced with explicit per-variant rename to compact form). 11 always-on + 9 Postgres-gated tests green. HardGate signed-envelope release explicitly forward-declared to MT-188 — acceptable scope split.
